data.title: Vivakor 2025 10-K: $104.4M Revenue, $(318.01) EPS on $110.2M Net Loss
data.description:
Vivakor reported $104.419 million in revenue for the year, a 16% increase driven by full-year operations from the Endeavor acquisition and expanded supply and trading activity, while consolidated net loss widened to $(110.237) million, producing basic and diluted net loss per share of $(318.01). The company's gross profit improved to $37.746 million (up 258%), but operating results were weighed down by a $40.570 million goodwill impairment and significant interest and debt conversion charges. These items together drove the large operating and net loss despite revenue and margin gains.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $104.419 million, increase of $14.608 million (16%) versus 2024.
  • Gross profit: $37.746 million, up $26.326 million (258%) versus 2024.
  • Loss from operations: $(63.828) million; included a $40.570 million goodwill impairment recorded in 2025.
  • Consolidated net loss: $(110.237) million; includes a $17.403 million loss on conversion of debt and $26.669 million of interest expense.
  • Basic and diluted net loss per share: $(318.01); weighted average common shares outstanding: 362,628.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Full-year operations from the Endeavor acquisition and the expansion of supply and trading contributed to the 16% increase in revenue.
  • Segment reorganization: Company reorganized into three operating segments—transportation & logistics, terminaling & storage, and supply & trading—to align with operational focus.
  • Transportation & logistics: Post-integration performance delivered strong margins and was a major contributor to gross profit improvement.
  • Supply & trading launch and scale-up: Launched in 2024 and scaled to $57.6 million of revenue in 2025, extending the company’s commercial reach.
  • Capital projects and asset optimization: Developing an RPC remediation facility in Texas and completed divestitures and asset realignments to optimize operations.

Original SEC Filing: Vivakor, Inc. [ VIVKD ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: QVC 2025 10-K: Revenue $9.23B, Operating loss $2.10B, Net loss $2.40B
data.description:
QVC reported consolidated net revenue of $9,230 million for the year, down 8.0% from the prior year, and posted a net loss of $2,398 million driven largely by non-cash impairments and restructuring charges; operating loss widened to $2,098 million. The company recorded an earnings (loss) before income taxes of $(2,583) million with a tax benefit of $185 million (effective tax rate ~7%). QVC has been investing in digital and streaming channels while executing operational restructurings to improve efficiency amid softer demand.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue, net: $9,230 million, decreased 8.0% vs prior year
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(2,098) million, operating loss increased $1,289 million (largely impairments)
  • Net earnings (loss): $(2,398) million, compared with $(1,250) million prior year
  • Earnings (loss) before income taxes: $(2,583) million; tax benefit $185 million (effective tax rate 7%)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Consolidated net revenue declined approximately 8% in 2025, driven by lower units shipped across all segments.
  • Channel and digital shift: Increased investment in digital platforms and social/streaming initiatives to broaden reach and drive engagement.
  • Brand and portfolio actions: Consolidated operations, sold St. Petersburg properties, and previously divested Zulily in 2023; tradename impairments were recorded.
  • Operational restructuring: Implemented the WIN strategy, consolidated studios, reorganized workforce, and shifted to managed services for IT to improve efficiency.
  • Supply and fulfillment: Completed Rocky Mount fire recovery, optimized fulfillment footprint; higher fulfillment costs and tariff-driven product costs negatively impacted margins.

Original SEC Filing: QVC Group, Inc. [ QVCGA ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Q/C Technologies 10-K: $0 Revenue, Net Loss $(11.59M)
data.description:
Q/C Technologies reported zero revenue for both 2025 and 2024 and recorded a net loss of $(11.587) million for the period, reflecting continued pre-revenue operations and investment activity. The company reported a loss from operations of $(9.807) million and a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $(14.944) million. Results reflect increased R&D and consulting spend as the company pivots its business model toward laser-based quantum-class computing solutions.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for 2025 and 2024.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(9.807) million (expense decreased 3.6% versus prior year).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(11.587) million, down 50.4% versus prior year.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: Net loss attributable to common stockholders of $(14.944) million, improved 45.0% versus prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic pivot: Transitioned from pharmaceutical operations to development of laser-based, quantum-class computing hardware (qc-LPU100) aimed at crypto and AI infrastructure applications.
  • Product development: Advancing the qc-LPU100 prototype with benchmarking, pursuing licensing collaborations (including with LightSolver), and planning certifications and pilot deployments.
  • Revenue model shift: Plans to monetize via hardware sales, leasing, subscription access to LPU clusters, and licensing/royalties for blockchain use cases.
  • Operational resourcing: Increased R&D program expenditures and external consulting; shifted to contractor-led development with a minimal full-time R&D headcount.
  • Corporate actions and talent: Entered executive and consulting agreements to strengthen deal sourcing, potential acquisitions, and commercialization capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: Q/C TECHNOLOGIES, INC. [ QCLS ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nauticus Robotics 10-K: Revenue $5.28M, Loss per Share $(10.45)
data.description:
Nauticus Robotics reported fiscal-year revenue of $5.275 million and an adjusted basic and diluted loss per share of $(10.45), reflecting revenue growth from a recent fleet acquisition while losses remained driven by integration and operating costs. The company narrowed its net loss to $(40.828) million versus the prior year, helped by lower extinguishment-of-debt and fair-value items, even as operating losses modestly increased. Management highlighted a shift from development to commercialization and integration of SeaTrepid operations to support future commercial and defense opportunities.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $5.275 million, up $3.467 million (192%) year over year, driven by SeaTrepid ROV fleet acquisition.
  • Operating loss: $(23.727) million, essentially flat year over year (2% increase in loss), reflecting higher costs and integration expenses.
  • Net loss: $(40.828) million, improved $94.079 million year over year (‑70%), benefited from lower loss on extinguishment of debt and changes in fair value.
  • Basic and diluted loss per share (As adjusted): $(10.45); weighted average shares 6.681 million (post reverse splits).
  • Cost of revenue: $12.337 million, up $2.604 million (27%).
  • Depreciation & amortization: $2.345 million, up 35%, contributing to gross and operating loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and drivers: Net revenue increased 192% YoY to $5.275 million primarily due to the acquisition of SeaTrepid’s ROV fleet, expanding service capability.
  • Channel and market focus: Strategic emphasis shifted toward commercial offshore energy and defense markets, leveraging the SeaTrepid acquisition to broaden service offerings.
  • Product commercialization: Achieved technological feasibility and transitioned R&D from development to market deployment of AUVs and robotic systems.
  • Operational integration: Integrated SeaTrepid operations and intangible assets, which increased depreciation and G&A from acquisition-related costs during the period.
  • Market momentum and outlook: Prioritizing oil & gas and defense segments while pursuing broader subsea commercialization opportunities to drive future growth.

Original SEC Filing: Nauticus Robotics, Inc. [ KITT ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nature's Miracle Holding 2025 10-K — Revenue $1.74M, Net Loss $11.99M
data.description:
Nature's Miracle Holding reported steeply reduced revenue of $1.742 million for the fiscal year, reflecting constrained inventory purchases that limited sales, and recorded a net loss of $11.987 million driven in part by higher interest expense and credit losses. Gross results showed a gross loss of $(0.451) million (a (25.9)% margin) while operating losses narrowed versus the prior year due to lower selling, general and administrative costs. The company did not report net income per share in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1.742 million, decreased 81.2% from $9.262 million in 2024 due to constrained inventory purchases limiting sales.
  • Gross loss: $(0.451) million; gross loss margin (25.9)%, improved from (30.3)% due to lower inventory write-offs.
  • Loss from operations: $(7.152) million, decreased 30.9% versus $(10.348) million in 2024, attributable to lower SG&A.
  • Net loss: $(11.987) million, decreased 12.2% versus $(13.653) million in 2024; interest expense and credit losses increased.
  • Net income per share: Not reported.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Sales declined roughly 81% in 2025 to $1.74 million as constrained inventory purchases limited the company's ability to fulfill demand.
  • Channel & product mix: Continued emphasis on commercial LED grow lights while expanding product offerings to include fertilizers, dehumidifiers and grow media to broaden its catalogue.
  • Brand and subsidiary strategy: Visiontech (eFinity) remains a core brand; Hydroman was renamed and repositioned to pursue EV truck distribution and mobile indoor growing initiatives.
  • Strategic diversification: Began investments in Future Tech (Bitcoin/data center) and entered a licensing arrangement for Datavault AI carbon tokenization, signaling diversification beyond core horticulture offerings.
  • Manufacturing & sourcing: Maintains long-term supplier relationships in Asia and Europe and reported strategic cooperation with Sinoinnovo to expand sourcing and manufacturing capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: Nature's Miracle Holding Inc. [ NMHI ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: My Size 2025 10-K: Revenue $9.36M, Net Loss $(5.85)M
data.description:
My Size reported fiscal 2025 revenues of $9.36 million and a net loss of $(5.85) million, reflecting growth in top-line sales largely from acquisitions and expanded marketplace channels while profitability was pressured by higher cost of revenues and increased operating expenses. Gross profit declined to $3.00 million and operating loss widened to $(5.74) million as the company absorbed acquisition-related costs and scaled G&A and R&D. The filing does not disclose net income per share in the provided sections.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $9.362 million, up from $8.257 million in 2024, driven primarily by Orgad and inclusion of acquisitions.
  • Gross profit: $3.000 million, down from $3.323 million in 2024 due to higher cost of revenues from consolidated acquisitions.
  • Operating loss: $(5.740) million, widened from $(3.944) million in 2024 reflecting higher G&A and acquisition-related costs.
  • Net loss: $(5.852) million, increased from $(3.995) million in 2024 driven by the operating loss and higher expenses.
  • Net income per share: Not disclosed in Part II Item 7/Item 8 (omitted per instructions).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and channel mix: Top-line growth to $9.36 million was driven by acquisitions and expanded sales channels, with Orgad’s Amazon and third-party marketplace sales materially contributing to revenue.
  • Acquisitions and platform expansion: The company integrated Naiz (size/fit technology), New Percentil (recommerce), ShoeSize.Me and Ten Peacks, broadening its platform capabilities across size, fit and recommerce services.
  • Operational scale-up: Increased investment in R&D, sales and marketing, and general and administrative functions to support product development, hiring and integration of acquired businesses.
  • Cash runway and capital plan: Management indicates cash runway is under 12 months and is pursuing an at-the-market equity offering to fund operations and growth initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: My Size, Inc. [ MYSZ ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Mitesco 2025 10-K: $38.7K Revenue, $0.04 Basic EPS
data.description:
Mitesco reported fiscal 2025 revenues of $38,700 and basic net income per share of $0.04, while recording an operating loss as operating expenses exceeded revenue. The company posted consolidated net income after taxes of $498,724 for the year despite the net operating loss, reflecting items outside operating results. Revenue declined slightly from the prior year and the company has shifted its operations and strategic focus toward new business units.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.039 million (Revenues of $38,700 for year ended Dec 31, 2025; down from $43,700 prior year)
  • Gross Profit: -$1.878 million (reported as Net Operating Loss; operating expenses exceeded revenue)
  • Operating Income: -$1.878 million (Net Operating Loss of $(1,878,033) for year ended Dec 31, 2025)
  • Net Income: $0.499 million (Consolidated net income after taxes of $498,724 for year ended Dec 31, 2025)
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic: $0.04; Diluted: $(0.14) (Basic net income per common share $0.04; diluted $(0.14))

Business Highlights

  • Business pivot: Transitioned from operating clinics and telemedicine services to functioning as a holding company with two new operating units: Centcore (data center services) and VTV (investments and AI development).
  • Operational buildout: Launched Centcore managed services, pursuing a small-format data center model and engaging experienced data center and cybersecurity contractors to support service delivery.
  • Revenue sources: Current revenue is low and principally derived from Centcore services and digital marketing activities that support development efforts.
  • Product development: VTV is developing an AI-based "Robo Agent" for real estate sales targeted for Q3 FY2026 and a sports/pickleball listing app planned for FY2026.
  • Cost and staffing actions: Closed unprofitable clinic operations (treated as discontinued) and relies on contractors to control staffing costs while building new operating capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: Mitesco, Inc. [ MITI ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: ENvue Medical 10-K: $2.55M revenue; $18.19M net loss
data.description:
ENvue Medical reported revenue of $2.553 million for the year, essentially flat with the prior year, while recording a net loss of $18.185 million driven by large impairments and increased operating expenses; diluted loss per share was not specified in the filing excerpts. Gross profit fell sharply to $0.153 million from the prior year, and operating results were weighed down by impairments, higher R&D, quality/regulatory and commercial investments.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $2.553 million, essentially flat year-over-year (2025: $2,553 vs 2024: $2,558).
  • Gross Profit: $0.153 million, decreased ~90% year-over-year due to product removal, inventory write-downs, and added amortization.
  • Operating Income: $(17.9) million, reflecting an operating loss driven by large impairments and increased R&D, S&M, and G&A.
  • Net Loss: $(18.185) million, a deterioration of 391% from $(3.705) million in the prior year.
  • Net Loss Per Share: Not explicitly stated in the provided filings.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Mix Shift: Revenue remained stable but shifted from PainShield products toward ENvue systems, changing margins and sales dynamics.
  • Product Removal and Inventory Impact: Discontinuation/removal of PainShield Ultra and related inventory write-downs materially reduced gross margins and profitability.
  • Commercialization: Continued ENvue commercialization with system installations and distributor sales; sales channels included the VA and workers’ compensation markets.
  • Operational Investment: Increased spending on R&D, quality/regulatory activities, and sales & marketing to support ENvue reactivation, address regulatory audits, and advance product development.
  • Impairment & Restructuring: Significant goodwill and intangible impairments tied to ENvue, reflecting a reassessment of expected future operating performance.

Original SEC Filing: ENvue Medical, Inc. [ FEED ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cirtran 2025 10-K: Revenue $3.13M, EPS $(0.14)
data.description:
Cirtran reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $3.13 million and a net loss per share of $(0.14), reflecting stronger vapor product sales that drove a 141.1% increase in revenue year-over-year while still producing an overall net loss of $(0.70) million. Gross profit improved to $1.54 million, and operating results were impacted by higher selling and marketing spend related to expanded retail distribution. The company noted operational progress on HUSTLER® product development and began generating sufficient cash for day-to-day operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $3.126891 million, up 141.1% versus prior year (driven by vapor product sales)
  • Gross profit: $1.540697 million, improved from $0.838638 million in the prior year
  • Loss from operations: $(0.129723) million; operating expenses increased due to higher marketing spend
  • Net loss: $(0.701634) million, improved from prior year net loss of $(2.626876) million
  • Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: $(0.14); weighted average shares outstanding: 4,945,417

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth: Reported revenue rose 141.1% to $3.13 million in 2025, driven by stronger sales in vapor products.
  • Channel momentum: Launched expanded retail chain distribution late in the year, prompting increased selling and marketing spend.
  • Brand development: Continued rollout of HUSTLER®-branded product lines under the 2019 manufacturing and distribution agreement.
  • Production & launches: Completed phases one and two of HUSTLER® product development, enabling broader product deliveries.
  • Operational cash flow: Operations used cash in 2025, but the company recently began generating enough cash for day-to-day operations.

Original SEC Filing: CIRTRAN CORP [ CIRX ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Ameritek Ventures 2025 10-K: Revenue $0, EPS $(1.90)
data.description:
Ameritek Ventures reported zero revenue for 2025 and a net loss per share of $(1.90), reflecting a strategic shift away from its prior software operations; this compares with $678,300 of revenue and EPS of $27.82 in 2024. The company recorded a large net loss of $(6.384392) million in 2025 driven by higher operating expenses, impairments and stock-based compensation, compared with net income of $13.518894 million the prior year. Management has pivoted operationally toward real estate and franchise activities during the year, which materially changed revenue sources and expense composition.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for 2025 (compared to $678,300 in 2024).
  • Operating income/(loss): $(2.709326) million operating loss in 2025 (versus $219,332 operating income in 2024).
  • Net income (loss): $(6.384392) million in 2025 (compared with net income of $13.518894 million in 2024).
  • Net income (loss) per common share: $(1.90) in 2025 (compared with $27.82 in 2024).

Business Highlights

  • Strategic pivot: Transitioned from a software-focused model to activities centered on real estate and franchising, changing the company’s primary operating channel.
  • New entities and operations: Established Chicago Real Estate Partners and became a fitness center franchisee, initiating franchise and real estate operations.
  • Investing activity: Deployed capital in 2025 toward franchise purchases and real estate acquisitions to build the new business channel.
  • Operational impact: Restructuring and the shift in business model drove higher operating expenses, including asset impairments and stock-based compensation tied to the transition.
  • Liquidity focus: Continued reliance on cash equivalents and short-term funding to support early-stage real estate and franchise growth initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: Ameritek Ventures, Inc. [ ATVK ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Mannatech 2025 10-K: Revenue $108.0M, EPS $(8.00)
data.description:
Mannatech reported consolidated net sales of $108.0 million for the fiscal year, a decline versus the prior year, and recorded a net loss of $15.2 million which translated to diluted net loss per share of $(8.00), driven largely by significant non‑cash income tax charges. Gross profit and margins contracted versus the prior year due to higher product and freight costs and operational disruptions. Management has initiated cost, staffing and margin actions to restore cash flow and operational stability.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $108.0 million, decreased $9.9 million (8.3%) versus 2024; $8.0 million (6.8%) decline on a constant dollar basis.
  • Gross profit: $81.0 million, decreased $10.5 million (11.5%); gross margin 74.9% versus 77.6% in 2024, reflecting higher product and freight costs.
  • Operating (loss) income: $(0.4) million, compared with $1.4 million in 2024; on a constant dollar basis operating income was $0.1 million.
  • Net (loss) income: $(15.2) million, driven by $12.3 million of non‑cash income tax charges, including $11.5 million of deferred tax expense.
  • Net income per share (diluted): $(8.00) per diluted share, versus $1.32 per diluted share in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Consolidated net sales fell 8.3% to $108.0 million in 2025, driven by lower orders across regions.
  • Channel and regional mix: International sales increased as a percentage of total to 69.9%; Americas sales declined 18.1% amid fewer active associates.
  • Associate base and orders: Active associate count decreased from 133,000 to 114,000; continuing associates also fell, reducing order volume and average order value.
  • Operational challenges and remediation: Supply‑chain cost increases and North America ERP issues negatively impacted revenue (approximate $6 million effect) and margins; management implemented process fixes to address these issues.
  • Cost and liquidity actions: Management executed cost reductions, headcount reductions, director fee‑to‑equity conversions and margin initiatives aimed at restoring cash flow.

Original SEC Filing: MANNATECH INC [ MTEX ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: LQR 2025 10-K: $1.565M Revenue, $(25.523)M Net Loss
data.description:
LQR reported total revenues of $1.565 million for the year, down 37% from 2024 as the company reduced marketing spend to prioritize profitability over growth; gross profit improved to $0.169 million from a prior-year loss. Despite operating improvements that reduced the loss from operations to $(11.429) million, LQR recorded a net loss of $(25.523) million, driven largely by a $13.0 million legal settlement. Net income per share was not provided in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $1.565 million, down 37% vs 2024 due to lower product sales from reduced marketing spend.
  • Gross profit (loss): $0.169 million, improved from a $(0.313) million loss in 2024 due to lower product fulfillment costs.
  • Loss from operations: $(11.429) million, improved from $(18.487) million in 2024 driven by higher gross profit and lower operating expenses.
  • Net loss: $(25.523) million, increased vs $(22.754) million in 2024 largely due to a $13.0 million legal settlement.
  • Net income per share: Not provided in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Product revenues fell approximately 39% in 2025 as the company cut marketing spend to prioritize profitability over growth.
  • Channel shift: Reduced customer acquisition and advertising on the CWS e‑commerce platform led to lower order volume.
  • Brand momentum: Continued development and sales of proprietary SWOL Tequila and planned Soleil Vino subscription efforts.
  • Strategic investments: Funded MCN joint ventures in late 2025 (20% stakes) to expand digital content reach; advances were returned in 2026.
  • Operational discipline: Lower operating and fulfillment costs in 2025 drove a positive gross profit versus a 2024 gross loss.

Original SEC Filing: LQR House Inc. [ YHC ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Atlantic International 10-K: $435.9M Revenue, $(1.08) EPS
data.description:
Atlantic International reported service revenue of $435.9 million for the year, down $6.7 million (1.5%) year‑over‑year, and posted a net loss per share of $(1.08) as weighted‑average shares were 54.85 million. Gross profit was $46.0 million and the company recorded an operating loss of $(50.23) million driven by higher SG&A net loss improved to $(59.43) million reflecting fewer one‑time merger and other charges. Management expects the recently acquired Circle8 to strengthen liquidity, operating efficiencies and revenue stability going forward.

Financial Highlights

  • Service revenue, net: $435.9 million, down $6.7M (1.5%) YoY, primarily due to lower temporary placement revenues.
  • Gross profit: $46.0 million, down $1.2M (2.5%) YoY; gross margin approximately 10.6% versus 10.7% prior year.
  • (Loss) income from operations: $(50.23) million, worsened by $28.4M YoY driven by higher selling, general & administrative expenses.
  • Net loss: $(59.43) million, improved by $76.05M YoY reflecting fewer one‑time merger and other charges.
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(1.08); weighted‑average shares outstanding: 54.85 million (2025).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and trends: Temporary placements remain the core of the business (over 99% of revenue); permanent placements grew roughly 18% year‑over‑year.
  • Client dynamics: Modest overall revenue decline was driven primarily by lower temporary staffing revenue from the company's largest client.
  • Geographic and scale expansion: National footprint with more than 100 U.S. locations; acquisition of Circle8 intended to broaden scale and international reach.
  • Operational focus: Emphasis on proactive recruitment, workforce management solutions and onsite local support to improve fulfillment and client service.
  • Outlook: Management expects the Circle8 combination to enhance liquidity, deliver operating efficiencies and support revenue stability going forward.

Original SEC Filing: ATLANTIC INTERNATIONAL CORP. [ ATLN ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Bitcoin traders cash out 63K BTC profit as price rallied above $76K: Will the market rebound?
data.description:
Bitcoin’s (BTC) rally stalled above $76,000 stalled on Tuesday after short-term profit-taking by traders reached its highest level in 2026. 

The activity coincided with continued accumulation by long-term holders, and this opposing interaction between the two cohorts may continue to impact Bitcoin’s attempts to break into the $80,000 range.

Bitcoin profit-taking meets whale demand

New Bitcoin short-term holders moved their holdings as BTC in profit sent to exchanges reached 63,000 BTC on April 14, the highest level in 2026, since the 44,800 spike on Jan. 14.

Onchain data shows that the one-day-to-one-week cohort moved nearly 2,000 BTC back to Binance during the same time. This implied that freshly acquired coins are rotating into sell-side liquidity as BTC traded near $76,000.

Crypto analyst Amr Taha flagged this as the first clear wave of profit-taking after the retest of the monthly highs. The activity aligns with cautious distribution, in which newer participants seek to secure gains at key resistance levels during a bear market.

Taha noted that this indicates a natural cooling phase in momentum.

Meanwhile, BTC whale behavior shows a different pattern. Market analyst CW noted a single-day inflow of over 71,000 BTC into accumulation addresses, the largest bullish inflow since early 2022. The large holders appear to be absorbing available supply from the short-term sellers. 

The relationship between these flows points to a transfer of coins from weaker hands to stronger ones, which may stabilize the price while limiting an immediate rally.

Related: Bitcoin ETFs post $412M in inflows as Goldman Sachs files for BTC ETF

Bitcoin liquidity cluster may lead to a small dip

After forming equal highs near $76,000, BTC’s price rejected near the 100-day exponential moving average (EMA), marking the first test of this trend since Jan. 14. The momentum slowed after the rejection, with price slipping to $73,500.

However, on the lower time frame, the bullish trend remains intact. 

On the one-hour chart, internal liquidity levels are resting around $73,000 and $72,000. These zones may attract bid orders that may get filled before a trend continuation.

The liquidation heatmap provides additional context, with $1.4 billion in cumulative long liquidations clustered around $73,000. That figure rises to $3.5 billion in long positions at risk near $70,500.

At the opposite end, a move toward $80,000 would expose $2 billion in leveraged short positions. The spread between the long and short liquidation zones suggests BTC may retest the $72,000 to $70,000 range before moving higher. 

Related: Bitcoin shows ‘bull market behavior’ as chart pattern targets $90K

data.title: ECB in 'relatively favorable' policy position, Schnabel says
data.description:
By Leika Kihara

The European Central Bank is in a "relatively favorable" position on monetary policy after having successfully brought down inflation to 2% before the war in the Middle East started, ECB board member Isabel Schnabel said on Wednesday.

"We have a monetary policy stance that is broadly neutral and this means we can afford to take the time that is needed to analyze the character of this shock. We do not need to rush into action," she said during a panel at the Institute of International Finance in Washington.

The current energy shock is particularly problematic because it has occurred after a very long period of above-target inflation, keeping memories of high inflation very fresh in the public's mind and inflation expectations more fragile, she said.

As a net energy importer, however, the euro zone economy may weaken from rising fuel prices, making it harder for firms to pass through costs or for workers to negotiate higher pay, Schnabel added.

"We have to weigh our policy decisions very carefully and see which ... prevails. We have to stay data-dependent. We have to think very carefully which data can give us information that inflation could become entrenched, and there could be second-round effects," she said.

But the ECB must also avoid imposing an "unnecessary cost" on the economy through premature monetary tightening, she said.

data.title: Former Apple Exec to Leave Ford. The Stock Is Down. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

It's tough to change century-old companies. The latest personnel announcement from Ford Motor is just the latest example.

Wednesday evening, Ford announced that Doug Field, chief EV, digital, and design officer, was leaving the company after almost five years.

The move came as Ford created a new "product creation and industrialization organization to scale next-generation vehicles and technology." Chief Operating Officer Kumar Galhotra will lead the new group. Alan Clarke was named vice president of Advanced Development Projects.

One of those projects is Ford's universal electric vehicle platform unveiled in 2025. The platform should help Ford produce more affordable EVs. New vehicles could arrive in 2027.

Field arrived in 2021, coming from Apple, where he was a vice president of special projects, likely working on Apple's car project, which never came to fruition. Before Apple, Field was a senior vice president of Engineering at Tesla, leading the development of the Model 3.

The hire was a coup for Ford. Back then, the lines between cars and phones were blurring. Cars were coming along with more computers and modems, and controlled by software that could be updated over the air.

(Shares outperformed the S&P 500 by about 1% the day following Ford's announcement.)

Electric vehicles were also all the rage. Tesla sales grew 87% in 2021, to more than 900,000 vehicles, and grew another 40% in 2022. Tesla's growth and its clean-sheet automotive design emphasizing software and touch screens sent shock waves through the industry, with car makers looking to add software-savvy tech executives.

To be sure, cars are smarter and millions of EVs have been sold. The overall structure of the industry, however, hasn't changed all that much.

Ford generated $10 billion in 2021 operating profit and similar amounts from 2022 through 2024. Higher tariff costs in 2025 sent operating profit down to about $6.8 billion. Operating profit is expected to recover to roughly $10 billion by 2028.

The car business remains cyclical, competitive, with lower-than-technology profits and profit margins.

Those are reasons Ford stock trades for about 7 times estimated 2027 earnings, almost the exact same multiple of forward earnings the stock fetched five years ago.

Ford stock was down 1.3% in after-hours trading. Shares were unchanged in regular trading, closing at $12.71. The S&P 500 added 0.8%.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Allegiant secures DOT interim exemption, clearing final regulatory hurdle for Sun Country acquisition
data.description:
Allegiant secured DOT interim exemption allowing both airlines to operate under common ownership after closing, clearing the last regulatory approval-related condition for the proposed Sun Country acquisition.

Key Highlights:

  • DOT granted the joint interim exemption permitting Allegiant and Sun Country to continue operating as separate carriers under common ownership post-closing.
  • The exemption preserves each airline’s business model and route networks while they advance toward a single operating certificate.
  • This DOT approval satisfies the last regulatory approval-related condition to closing; shareholder votes remain required.
  • Special shareholder meetings are scheduled for May 8, 2026; closing could occur as early as May 13, 2026 if remaining conditions are met or waived.

Original SEC Filing: Allegiant Travel CO [ ALGT ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Tidal Wave Auto Spa Announces Grand Opening in North Augusta, South Carolina
data.description:
Fifth Largest Conveyor Car Wash Company Celebrates Opening of 26th South Carolina Location with Free Washes, Membership Specials, and Giveback Day

THOMASTON, GA / ACCESS Newswire / April 15, 2026 / Tidal Wave Auto Spa, one of the nation's fastest-growing express car wash companies, is pleased to announce the grand opening of its North Augusta, SC location at 110 Merovan Drive.

To celebrate the Grand Opening, the brand-new North Augusta location is offering 12 days of free premium car washes from April 15 to April 26. This limited-time promotion lets customers experience the company's premium wash option, Graph-X4 + Super Shammy, at no cost. Additionally, any new customer who joins a Tidal Wave Clean Club unlimited wash membership during the Grand Opening celebration will get the first month of any unlimited wash plan for $9.97, for savings of up to $40.

"Our team is eager to share the Tidal Wave car wash experience with folks in North Augusta," said Tidal Wave Auto Spa CEO and Founder, Scott Blackstock. "As we celebrate the grand opening, we look forward to connecting with the community and providing easy, efficient, and enjoyable car care for years to come."

Tidal Wave is proud to invest in the communities it calls home, having donated more than $8 million to charitable organizations nationwide. In celebration of the North Augusta grand opening, the company will host its Giveback Day on Thursday, April 23rd, donating $1 for every free wash and $5 for every new Tidal Wave Clean Club membership to The RECing Crew.

President and Founder of The RECing Crew, Pam Stickler, expressed her enthusiasm for the event, "We are very excited to partner with Tidal Wave Auto Spa for the Grand Opening Giveback Day. This partnership helps us to bring affordable leisure and recreational programs for those with special needs within our community."

North Augusta, SC Location: 110 Merovan Drive, North Augusta, SC 29860

Nearby Locations: Augusta, GA, Evans, GA, Aiken, SC

Tidal Wave proudly serves customers at 316 express wash locations across the United States, including 26 in South Carolina. The company will open new locations in Indian Land and Duncan later this year, alongside openings in Alabama, Florida, North Carolina, Georgia, and more.

Tidal Wave Auto Spa is committed to providing every customer with an exceptional car wash experience through industry-leading car care technology, clean and welcoming locations, and friendly customer service. With single wash options starting at $15, unlimited car wash memberships and family plans, and fleet plans for businesses, Tidal Wave delivers wash options to fit every need.

For additional information about Tidal Wave Auto Spa, please visit: https://www.tidalwaveautospa.com/.

About Tidal Wave Auto Spa

Tidal Wave Auto Spa was founded over 20 years ago in Thomaston, GA, by husband and wife, Scott and Hope Blackstock. What started as a small-town self-service car wash business evolved into the first conveyor car wash open in Georgia and is now the fifth-largest conveyor car wash company in the nation, with 316 locations spanning 30 states. Tidal Wave is dedicated to delivering an exceptional car wash experience for every customer through industry-leading car care technology, clean and inviting locations, and outstanding customer service. The company is equally committed to making a positive difference in the communities it serves, raising over $8 million for local programs, service organizations, and non-profit organizations through its fundraising program and annual Charity Day event.

Contact Information: media@tidalwaveautospa.com

SOURCE: Tidal Wave Auto Spa

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Thinkific CEO Greg Smith to Take Direct Supervision of Product and Technology Teams to Accelerate AI-Driven Innovation
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC, April 15, 2026 /CNW/ - Thinkific Labs Inc. ("Thinkific" or the "Company") , a leading learning commerce platform, today announced that CEO Greg Smith is expanding his responsibilities by taking direct oversight of the R&D teams, replacing Ryan Donovan who will be leaving Thinkific to pursue other opportunities. This move is designed to accelerate Thinkific's AI-driven product innovation by streamlining decision-making and shortening time-to-market. In this expanded capacity, Smith will personally oversee the execution of Thinkific's core technology and product strategies. Thinkific has strong VPs leading core R&D functions to help lead and execute in this area. Their focus will be on rapid innovation and customer value creation through the use of AI tools.

Greg and the team at Thinkific extend their sincere thanks to Ryan Donovan for his many contributions and for helping to hire and build the team and product foundations that will enable the company's future success.

About Thinkific

Thinkific is an award-winning learning commerce platform where courses and community come together to power business growth. Thinkific gives academies, experts, and businesses everything they need to create and sell online learning experiences, build communities, and grow their revenue — all from one platform. More than 35,000 customers — including companies like GoDaddy, Nasdaq, ActiveCampaign, and Datadog — have generated billions in revenue using Thinkific, impacting more than 200 million people worldwide.

Forward-Looking Information

This press release contains "forward-looking information" within the meaning of applicable Canadian securities laws. Forward-looking information includes, but is not limited to, statements regarding Thinkific's plans to accelerate AI-driven product innovation, the expected benefits of the CEO's expanded oversight of the Company's product and technology teams, the Company's core technology and product strategies, and the Company's plans to pursue rapid innovation and customer value creation through the use of AI tools. Forward-looking information is based on management's current expectations and assumptions and is subject to a number of risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described in the forward-looking information. These risks and uncertainties include, among others, general economic conditions, competitive factors, regulatory changes, and other factors described in the Company's annual information form and other filings with Canadian securities regulators available on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. Forward-looking information is provided as of the date of this press release, and Thinkific disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking information, whether as a result of new information, future events, or otherwise, except as required by applicable law.

SOURCE Thinkific Labs Inc.

data.title: SVRA Stock Slips Post-Market After FDA Delays Decision On Pulmonary Therapy
data.description:
Key points:
  • The company was looking for FDA’s greenlight for molgramostim in Autoimmune Pulmonary Alveolar Proteinosis.
  • The company had submitted certain responses to the FDA recently which the agency determined represents a major amendment to the application, the company explained.
  • Savara added that the agency did not cite any safety, efficacy, or manufacturing concerns in their correspondence.

Shares of Savara Inc. (SVRA) slumped as much as 4% after-hours on Wednesday after the company said that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has extended the review time of its application seeking approval for its experimental therapy molgramostim by three months.

The company was looking for the FDA’s greenlight for molgramostim in Autoimmune Pulmonary Alveolar Proteinosis (Autoimmune PAP), a rare lung disease where autoantibodies block the clearing of surfactant in the lungs, causing a buildup that disrupts oxygen exchange, creating breathing difficulties, and chronic coughing.

The agency is now slated to decide on the application by November 22, Savara said. The former deadline for a decision was August 22.

Why The Delay?

The company had submitted certain responses to the FDA recently, which the agency determined represents a major amendment to the application, the company explained. The additional time will allow the agency time to review the extra materials submitted, it added.

Savara added that the agency did not cite any safety, efficacy, or manufacturing concerns in its correspondence.

Savara completed submission of its application to the FDA for molgramostim as a treatment for aPAP in March. It was granted priority review by the agency. According to Savara, molgramostim has the potential to be the first and only approved therapy for Autoimmune PAP.

The company had previously submitted an application for the experimental therapy but received a “refuse to file” letter from the FDA in May 2025 due to insufficient Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls data. The company resubmitted the application, stating that Fujifilm Biotechnologies will manufacture the drug substance.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around SVRA stock stayed within the ‘neutral’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘low’ levels.

SVRA stock has more than doubled over the past 12 months.
